symptom: AKS cluster autoscaler exhibits erratic scale-up/scale-down fluctuation on K8s 1.24 preview; node count oscillates rapidly (ICM-324784882)
rootCause: Known bug in cluster autoscaler on K8s 1.24 preview causing rapid oscillation between scale-up and scale-down decisions
solution: Upgrade to GA version of 1.24 or later. Workaround: set autoscaler profile with --scale-down-delay-after-add and --scale-down-unneeded-time to prevent rapid oscillation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_08_11.md

symptom: AKS API server completely unreachable; 2 of 3 etcd instances restarted simultaneously causing all 3 API server pods to go offline
rootCause: Simultaneous etcd restart (2/3 replicas) caused etcd quorum loss, leading to cascading API server pod failure
solution: Control plane issue requiring PG investigation via ICM. Verify uptime SLA is enabled. For large clusters ensure control plane tier matches workload requirements.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_08_24.md

symptom: App Gateway with AKS internal LB IP as backend; cert rotation may change iLB IP breaking App Gateway health probes
rootCause: AKS cert rotation triggers node reimaging which may recreate internal LB, potentially changing iLB IP if not using static IP
solution: Workaround: 1) Rotate CCP certs only; 2) Scale up new nodes; 3) Cordon/drain old nodes; 4) Delete old nodes. Or configure iLB with static IP.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_08_24.md

symptom: AKS worker nodes experience periodic high IO spikes; updatedb/mlocate cron job consuming excessive disk IO, impacting pod performance
rootCause: Ubuntu mlocate package runs daily cron (updatedb.mlocate) scanning entire filesystem. On AKS nodes with many container layers this causes significant IO overhead.
solution: Disable mlocate cron: chmod -x /etc/cron.daily/mlocate or remove the package. For AKS Engine add custom script extension. Ref: https://github.com/Azure/aks-engine/issues/2971
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_09_08.md

symptom: AKS nodes using kubenet with /24 pod CIDR report InsufficientPodCidr error; pods stuck in Pending, cannot schedule new pods
rootCause: With kubenet each node gets /24 subnet from pod CIDR range. When total nodes exceed available subnets, pod CIDR space is exhausted.
solution: Use larger pod CIDR range (e.g. /14 or /12) at cluster creation with --pod-cidr. Cannot change for existing clusters - must recreate. Consider Azure CNI.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_09_22.md

symptom: AKS upgrade with Availability Zones causes temporary zone imbalance; surge nodes placed in unknown zones
rootCause: AKS uses best-effort zone balancing in VMSS. During upgrade surge, zone for surge nodes is unknown, causing temporary imbalance until surge nodes deleted
solution: Set upgrade surge to multiple of 3 nodes so VMSS can balance across AZs. Original zone balance restored after upgrade
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_10_20.md

symptom: AKS cluster creation or upgrade fails when PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) is enabled on K8s 1.25+
rootCause: PSP removed in K8s 1.25. AKS blocks creation/upgrade for PSP-enabled clusters on v1.25+
solution: Migrate from PSP to Pod Security Admission (PSA) or Azure Policy before upgrading to 1.25+
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2022_12_14.md

symptom: AKS cluster with Calico network policy should not upgrade to K8s 1.25; may cause networking failures
rootCause: Calico has compatibility issues with K8s 1.25 changes (cgroups v2, PSP removal, API deprecations)
solution: Check Calico compatibility matrix before upgrading. Update Calico to 1.25-compatible version or use Azure Network Policy
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-01-12.md

symptom: AKS cluster unexpectedly stopped; cluster with 0 Ready nodes or all NotReady auto-stopped after 30 days
rootCause: Since Jan 3 2023, AKS expanded auto-stop: clusters with 0 Ready nodes, all NotReady, or 0 running VMs auto-stopped after 30 days
solution: Run az aks start to restart. Avoid leaving clusters with all nodes stopped/NotReady >30 days
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-01-12.md

symptom: AKS autoscaler triggers false Service Health/Resource Health alerts showing unexpected VM restarts; VMSS ForceDelete in CRP logs
rootCause: Autoscaler uses ForceDelete for scaled-down VMSS instances. ForceDelete creates Resource Health annotation appearing as unplanned restart. Owned by Service Health team
solution: Verify via VMApiQosEvent (operationName=VirtualMachineScaleSets.ForceDelete.POST with AKS RP user agent). ADO: msazure/One/_workitems/edit/17378188
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-03-09.md

symptom: After AKS upgrade to K8s 1.25+, memory utilization significantly higher; pods OOM killed, especially .NET 3.1 apps
rootCause: K8s 1.25+ uses cgroups v2 (Ubuntu 22.04) with different memory accounting. Apps optimized for cgroups v1 may exceed limits. GitHub: Azure/AKS/issues/3443
solution: Increase memory limits for affected pods. Review .NET cgroups v2 compatibility. Track ICM 369500313, 373049776, 370740949
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-03-09.md

symptom: After AKS Stop/Start on K8s 1.26, ghost pods appear running on non-existent nodes
rootCause: Upstream K8s beta feature + podGC behavior change in 1.26 caused stale pod records after stop/start
solution: Fixed by AKS team. Upgrade to latest 1.26 patch. ICM: 390020923
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-05-18.md

symptom: Dapr extension not available on AKS in Mooncake; az k8s-extension create --extension-type Microsoft.Dapr fails
rootCause: Dapr extension not GA in Mooncake. No regions supported. No ETA (ICM 505882649, 2024-05-24)
solution: Use self-managed Dapr via Helm chart as workaround. Monitor AKS release notes for Mooncake availability
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-2-9.md

symptom: After AKS upgrade from K8s 1.23 to 1.24, nginx ingress health probes fail; Azure LB marks backend unhealthy
rootCause: K8s 1.24 breaking changes affect nginx ingress + Azure LB health probes. GitHub: kubernetes/ingress-nginx/issues/9538
solution: Add annotation service.beta.kubernetes.io/azure-load-balancer-health-probe-request-path to nginx service
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-2-9.md

symptom: Ubuntu 18.04 EOL on AKS nodes; customers on AKS <1.25 need migration plan
rootCause: Ubuntu 18.04 support ended May 2023. AKS continued images until July 2023 for K8s 1.24. K8s 1.25+ uses Ubuntu 22.04
solution: Upgrade to AKS 1.25+ for Ubuntu 22.04. For 1.24, run az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only for latest patched 18.04
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-03-09.md

symptom: After upgrading AKS to Kubernetes 1.24+, ServiceAccount token secrets are no longer automatically generated; pods relying on auto-mounted SA tokens may fail authentication
rootCause: Starting from K8s 1.24, automatic Secret creation for ServiceAccounts was removed. The legacy behavior of auto-creating kubernetes.io/service-account-token secrets is no longer supported.
solution: Manually create a Secret of type kubernetes.io/service-account-token with annotation kubernetes.io/service-account.name pointing to the SA. Or use kubectl create token <SA-name> for short-lived tokens. Ref: K8s 1.24 changelog urgent upgrade notes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY23/2023-4-20.md

symptom: AKS nodes running unattended Canonical nightly security updates causing unexpected disruptions; after Sept 2023, AKS disabled unmanaged nightly updates for clusters without explicit node OS upgrade channel set
rootCause: AKS disabled unmanaged Canonical nightly security updates by Sept 2023 to prevent uncontrolled node restarts. Clusters without an explicit node OS upgrade channel setting default to None.
solution: Proactively set node OS upgrade channel to NodeImage or SecurityPatch. Use az aks update --node-os-upgrade-channel. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/auto-upgrade-node-image
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2023-09-07.md

symptom: Azure Blob NFS mount on AKS pods fails or is misconfigured; NFS 3.0 requests cannot use account key, AAD, or ACL authorization; mount requires specific network configuration
rootCause: NFS 3.0 protocol is keyless - it does not support account key authorization, Microsoft Entra security, or ACLs. NFS 3.0 storage account must be configured with private endpoint or selected VNET access only. AKS 1.27+ supports IP address changes for Blob NFS mounts via aznfs mount helper.
solution: 1) Enable private endpoint or selected VNET for the storage account. 2) Install aznfs mount helper package on AKS 1.27+ for IP address change support. 3) Use no_root_squash carefully (security risk). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/network-file-system-protocol-support-how-to
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2023-10-19.md

symptom: Konnectivity-agent pod not running or failing health probes; need to debug konnectivity pod status transitions via kube-audit logs in Kusto
rootCause: Konnectivity pod lifecycle issues can be diagnosed via ControlPlaneEvents/ControlPlaneEventsNonShoebox tables in akscn.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/AKSccplogs. Filter by category kube-audit, search for konnectivity-agent in pods, then mv-expand requestObject.status.conditions.
solution: KQL: union cluster(akscn...).database(AKSccplogs).ControlPlaneEvents, ...NonShoebox | where ccpNamespace == '<underlay-id>' | where category == 'kube-audit' | where properties contains 'konnectivity-agent' | mv-expand podCond = _jlog.requestObject.status.conditions | project PreciseTimeStamp, requestURI, verb, user, podCondType, podCondStatus, podCondReason, podCondMessage
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2023-11-02.md

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade automatically stopped when deprecated/breaking API usage detected; upgrade blocked with API breaking change warning after release 2023-10-08
rootCause: AKS release 2023-10-08+ includes auto-stop feature that halts cluster upgrade when deprecated K8s API usage is detected via kube-audit logs. This prevents upgrades that would break workloads using removed APIs.
solution: Mitigate by updating workloads to use non-deprecated APIs, then resume upgrade. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/stop-cluster-upgrade-api-breaking-changes. Check deprecated API usage before upgrading.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2023-11-02.md

symptom: AKS nodes running Mariner OS encounter OS-level issues; CSS cannot troubleshoot Mariner OS internals directly
rootCause: Mariner (CBL-Mariner/Azure Linux) is a Microsoft-owned Linux distribution. OS-level issues in Mariner nodes are owned by the Mariner PG team, not the AKS support team.
solution: For Mariner OS-level issues, directly raise ICM to Mariner PG team. AKS support handles AKS-specific issues but Mariner kernel/package issues are escalated to Mariner PG.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2023-11-02.md

symptom: AKS kube-config becomes invalid/expired after platform-initiated certificate rotation; kubectl commands fail with certificate errors
rootCause: AKS platform performs automatic certificate rotation (ref: release 2023-05-14). After platform cert rotation, previously downloaded kubeconfig credentials become invalid because they contain old certificates. GitHub issue: Azure/AKS#3753.
solution: Re-download kubeconfig using az aks get-credentials after certificate rotation. Docs: https://docs.azure.cn/en-us/aks/certificate-rotation. Monitor cert expiry proactively.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2023-8-24.md

symptom: Kubelogin errors when connecting to AAD-integrated AKS cluster; authentication fails after upgrade or fresh setup in Mooncake environment
rootCause: AAD-integrated AKS clusters require kubelogin for authentication after K8s 1.24+ (in-tree Azure auth plugin removed). In Mooncake (21Vianet), kubelogin configuration may need China-specific AAD endpoint settings. LTS support confirmed working in Mooncake.
solution: Install kubelogin and configure for Mooncake AAD endpoints. Use kubelogin convert-kubeconfig after az aks get-credentials. Ensure kubelogin version supports China cloud.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2024_01_11.md

symptom: KEDA addon version remains at old version (e.g. 2.7.0) after upgrading AKS cluster from 1.25 to 1.26.10, instead of auto-upgrading to version matching release notes (expected 2.10.1)
rootCause: KEDA addon auto-upgrade behavior inconsistency during AKS cluster version upgrade; some clusters do not trigger KEDA version bump as expected per release notes
solution: File ICM for investigation; verify expected KEDA version from AKS release notes vs actual; normal behavior is KEDA auto-upgrades to match cluster version mapping in release notes
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2024_02_22.md

symptom: Various issues (e.g., IP lease problems in Load Balancer) after deleting or modifying platform-assigned managed tags in AKS node resource group (MC_*)
rootCause: AKS platform-managed tags are used internally for resource lifecycle management; removing or modifying them breaks AKS resource reconciliation
solution: Do NOT delete/modify platform-assigned managed tags in node resource group. Use 'az aks update --tags' for custom tags. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-tags
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2024_03_07.md

symptom: AKS cluster creation with system-managed identity fails when subnet has UDR associated (kubenet); Portal also blocks creation for CNI; CLI error: OnlySupportedOnUserAssignedMSICluster
rootCause: System-managed identity does not get role assignment on UDR/route table; kubenet with custom route tables requires user-assigned managed identity. Portal has stricter validation than CLI for CNI scenario
solution: Use user-assigned managed identity for kubenet with custom route tables (UDR). For Azure CNI with CLI, creation succeeds even with UDR on subnet (no route table role needed). Portal limitation requires removing UDR first or using CLI
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/2024_05_30.md

symptom: Customer cannot control AGIC add-on version independently; experienced issue with specific AGIC version but cannot upgrade without upgrading entire AKS cluster
rootCause: AGIC add-on is a managed service; version is tied to AKS cluster version with no independent version control. Auto-updated as managed service per Microsoft documentation
solution: Must upgrade AKS cluster to get newer AGIC add-on version. Alternative: use Helm deployment instead of add-on for manual version control. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/ingress-controller-overview
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###FY24/23-07-13.md

symptom: Customer requests Azure Container Storage on AKS in Mooncake; feature is not available
rootCause: Azure Container Storage has not been deployed to Mooncake (21Vianet) environment as of Nov 2024. The feature is only available in Azure public cloud regions.
solution: Inform customer Azure Container Storage is not yet available in Mooncake. Track feature landing status with AKS PG. Alternative: use Azure Disk or Azure Files CSI drivers for persistent storage.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/##Azure Container Storage.md

symptom: AKS pods fail to pull images with 401 Unauthorized (ImagePullBackOff) after Service Principal credential expires; ACR authentication fails
rootCause: AKS Service Principal credential expired. SP credential is written directly in /etc/kubernetes/azure.json on each node. Standard SP reset (az aks update-credentials) requires node reboot which causes downtime.
solution: Graceful SP update without node reboot: 1) SSH to AKS node, read current SP secret from /etc/kubernetes/azure.json; 2) Use az ad app credential reset --id <appId> --password <current-secret> --append to extend expiry (requires az cli <= 2.36.0); 3) No node reboot needed since AKS and VMSS config remain unchanged. Note: az cli breaking change (Graph migration) removed --password parameter in newer versions.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/##Graceful update SP.md

symptom: Customer asks about AKS Long Term Support (LTS) availability or pricing in Mooncake; confusion about which K8s versions support LTS
rootCause: From April 2025, all new AKS GA Kubernetes versions (starting 1.27+) are LTS-compatible. LTS requires Premium tier (~.5/day per cluster). LTS provides 1 additional year of CVE patches beyond community EOL.
solution: Inform customer: 1) LTS is available for K8s 1.27, 1.28, 1.29, 1.30 (1.31/1.32 coming); 2) Requires AKS Premium tier; 3) Only deployable via CLI (not portal as of Jun 2025); 4) Pricing: see azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/kubernetes-service/; 5) Each LTS version gets 1 extra year of support with CVE fixes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/##LTS.md

symptom: After switching AKS cluster authorization mode from Azure RBAC to Kubernetes RBAC, users lose access to the cluster
rootCause: Azure RBAC role assignments do not convert to Kubernetes RBAC role/role bindings. When switching to K8s RBAC, explicit K8s Roles and RoleBindings must be pre-configured. Conversely, switching from K8s RBAC to Azure RBAC is safe because existing K8s role bindings are preserved.
solution: Before switching from Azure RBAC to Kubernetes RBAC: 1) Create K8s RBAC Roles and RoleBindings for all users/groups. 2) Use 'kubectl get clusterrolebindings' to verify bindings exist. Note: Switching from K8s RBAC to Azure RBAC preserves existing K8s bindings. Best practices: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/fasttrack-for-azure/azure-kubernetes-service-rbac-options-in-practice/ba-p/3684275
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/##RBAC.md

symptom: AKS stopped cluster cannot be recovered; cluster state lost after being stopped for extended period; only start or delete operations work on stopped clusters
rootCause: AKS stop/start feature has a 12-month maximum state preservation limit; stopped clusters beyond 12 months lose their state and cannot be recovered; additionally, no operations (scale/upgrade) can be performed on stopped clusters except start or delete; feature only supported on VMSS-based clusters
solution: Ensure stopped AKS clusters are restarted within 12 months; use `az aks start --name <name> --resource-group <rg>` to restart; to perform scale/upgrade, must start the cluster first; feature requires VMSS-based cluster (docs: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/aks/start-stop-cluster)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/#AKS stop start.md

symptom: AKS cluster certificates expired causing API server communication failure or cluster inaccessibility; kube-config credentials no longer valid
rootCause: AKS clusters created before May 2019 have CA certificates expiring after 2 years; clusters created after May 2019 have CA certs expiring after 30 years; all other certificates signed by Cluster CA expire after 2 years; intermediate certificates were NOT auto-rotated during upgrades until AKS release 2021-07-15
solution: Run `az aks rotate-certs -g $RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME -n $CLUSTER_NAME` to manually rotate all certificates; since AKS release 2021-07-15, intermediate certificates are automatically rotated during version upgrade; check cluster creation date with `kubectl get nodes` to determine CA expiry timeline (docs: https://docs.azure.cn/en-us/aks/certificate-rotation)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/#Cert and SSH.md

symptom: Need to SSH into AKS VMSS worker node for troubleshooting; kubectl debug method for node access in Mooncake/21V environment
rootCause: AKS nodes have no direct SSH access by default; official documentation previously lacked the chroot /host step for kubectl debug method
solution: Method 1: kubectl-node-shell plugin (https://github.com/kvaps/kubectl-node-shell); Method 2 (official): `kubectl debug node/<node-id> -it --image=mcr.azk8s.cn/aks/fundamental/base-ubuntu:v0.0.11` then run `chroot /host` to access node filesystem (note: use mcr.azk8s.cn mirror for Mooncake)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/#Cert and SSH.md

symptom: AKS auto-upgrade triggers unexpectedly without notification; customer unclear about auto-upgrade behavior, scheduling, and maintenance window interaction
rootCause: AKS auto-upgrade has no pre-notification mechanism; once enabled and a new version is detected, upgrade begins immediately if no maintenance window is configured; auto-upgrade and planned maintenance work together only when maintenance window is explicitly set (minimum 4 hours recommended)
solution: Configure planned maintenance window (at least 4 hours) alongside auto-upgrade to control upgrade timing (docs: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/aks/planned-maintenance); set node surge to 33% for production (not 100% to avoid simultaneous node disruption); auto-upgrade without scheduler means upgrade happens as soon as new version detected; use `az aks update --auto-upgrade-channel` to set channel (docs: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/aks/auto-upgrade-cluster)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Feature Landing Status###/#Auto-Upgrade.md

symptom: Attempting to enable AKS Defender Profile in Mooncake (Azure China) fails; feature is not available
rootCause: AKS Defender Profile is a feature gap in Mooncake (China) cloud; the security profile is not supported
solution: Inform customer this is a known feature gap in Mooncake. Check latest feature availability for updates
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Defender for Container.md

symptom: AKS operation fails with error AzureDefenderFeatureFlagNotEnabled: AzureDefender installation is not allowed since feature Microsoft.ContainerService/AKS-AzureDefender is not enabled
rootCause: Feature flag Microsoft.ContainerService/AKS-AzureDefender is not registered/enabled in the subscription or region
solution: Disable the defender addon: 1) Disable in MDC settings; 2) Delete addon pods; or use CLI: az aks disable-addons --addons azure-policy as workaround
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Defender for Container/Disable MDC for containers.md

symptom: After removing Defender sensor from AKS cluster, CRDs (policies.defender.microsoft.com, runtimepolicies.defender.microsoft.com) still remain
rootCause: PG bug - CLI does not clean up Defender CRDs during sensor removal
solution: Manually delete CRDs: kubectl delete crd policies.defender.microsoft.com runtimepolicies.defender.microsoft.com (safe). PG fixing CLI. Ref: ICM 555510090
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Defender for Container/Disable MDC for containers.md

symptom: In Mooncake, user cannot disable Azure Policy add-on on the Azure Portal; the option is non-functional
rootCause: Mooncake portal limitation/bug - disable toggle for Azure Policy add-on does not work
solution: Use CLI workaround: az aks disable-addons --addons azure-policy --name <cluster> --resource-group <rg>. PG fixing portal. Ref: ICM 555841053
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Defender for Container/Disable MDC for containers.md

symptom: CSS members working on AKS can no longer request JIT at CustomerDataAdministrator level
rootCause: Permission change effective 05/30/2024 - CustomerDataAdministrator level JIT removed for CSS, retained only for EEE and PG
solution: Use new JIT process: 1) Submit ICM via template O2h2V3; 2) Request JIT via jitaccess.security.core.chinacloudapi.cn; 3) AKS team approves in ~15 min; 4) Receive SAW VM access email
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##New JIT Process.md

symptom: Blobfuse CSI volume mounted in Prometheus pod shows incorrect volume size (e.g. 31.3G instead of requested 50Gi); volume gradually fills up and Prometheus pod gets terminated because it cannot write data
rootCause: Blobfuse volume reported size is determined by the local cache/temp disk size on the AKS node, not the YAML spec or storage account capacity. Files with open file handles (like Prometheus WAL/TSDB) are cached on the temp disk and consume real disk space, while files without handles (uploaded via portal or dd) show 0 bytes in du output
solution: Do not use blobfuse CSI volumes for workloads that keep persistent file handles (e.g. Prometheus TSDB). Use Azure Disk or Azure Files instead. If blobfuse is required, increase node temp disk size or configure --cache-size-mb mount option to limit cache usage. Track: github.com/kubernetes-sigs/blob-csi-driver/issues/862
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Storage in AKS/[Blobfuse] unexpected volume size causing Promethe.md

symptom: AKS nodes experience connection timeout/reset around UTC 06:00-07:00; network restart detected in syslog; issue auto-mitigates but recurs on different nodes on subsequent days
rootCause: Ubuntu unattended-upgrades triggers systemd update (e.g. 237-3ubuntu10.56 to 10.57) which causes network service restart; waagent detects DHCP client restart and restores routing table; during restart window, pod network connectivity is disrupted
solution: Workaround 1: Disable at OS level - edit /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades, set Unattended-Upgrade to 0. Workaround 2: Deploy DaemonSet (github.com/Azure/AKS/blob/master/examples/unattended-upgrades/daemonset.yaml). Workaround 3: Use AKS auto-upgrade with node-image channel + maintenance windows. Note: upgrading K8s version does NOT fix this
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Ubuntu Unattended Update Issue.md

symptom: Need to SSH directly into AKS VMSS worker node when kubectl-node-shell or kubectl debug is not available
rootCause: AKS nodes have no direct SSH access by default; VMAccessForLinux extension can inject SSH public key into VMSS instances as alternative method
solution: 1) Create Linux VM in same VNET. 2) ssh-keygen. 3) az vmss extension set --name VMAccessForLinux --publisher Microsoft.OSTCExtensions --version 1.4 --protected-settings with username and ssh_key. 4) az vmss update-instances --instance-ids *. 5) ssh -i id_rsa azureuser@node_ip. Cleanup: az vmss extension delete --name VMAccessForLinux
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##直接登录VMSS.md

symptom: AKS storage mount capacity behavior is confusing; mounted volume size does not match YAML PVC capacity spec
rootCause: Different storage drivers have different capacity semantics: Azure Disk size equals actual provisioned disk size (matches YAML). Azure Blob (blobfuse) capacity depends on storage account limit, YAML capacity is ignored. Azure Files capacity depends on file share quota, YAML capacity is ignored
solution: Use correct storage class parameters for dynamic vs static provisioning. For blob/files mounts, do not rely on YAML capacity field for size limiting. Reference: docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/aks/azure-files-csi
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Storage in AKS.md

symptom: Container Insights ama-logs (ama-logs-rs) pod OOMKilled; monitoring addon pod restarts due to memory pressure
rootCause: ama-logs replicaset pod memory usage exceeds default limit. PG disabled the previous backend adjustment process — CSS can no longer escalate ICM to PG to adjust resource limits for ama-logs pods.
solution: Customer self-tunes via configmap: 1) kubectl get cm -n kube-system | grep container-azm-ms-agentconfig; 2) if not exists, download from raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/Docker-Provider/ci_prod/kubernetes/container-azm-ms-agentconfig.yaml; 3) uncomment and set: monitoring_max_event_rate=50000, backpressure_memory_threshold_in_mb=1500, upload_max_size_in_mb=10, upload_frequency_seconds=1, compression_level=0; 4) apply configmap. TSG wiki: supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki/1880808
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/#Container Insights/##Insight addon ama pod OOM.md

symptom: Customer asks about ETCD database size limit, monitoring threshold, and when to alert for ETCD storage usage in AKS
rootCause: ETCD DB has different size limits based on AKS tier: paid tier = 8 GiB, free tier = 4 GiB. Exceeding 2 GiB indicates cluster needs optimization and may experience performance degradation.
solution: Configure alert at 2 GiB (25% for paid, 50% for free). Reference: 1) RHC: Etcd is Running out of Storage Space in eng.ms/docs/.../etcd-customer-communication; 2) Etcd in AKS 101 at eng.ms/docs/.../etcd-in-aks-101. Best practice: monitor via Kubernetes control plane alerts per learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/best-practices-monitoring-proactive
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/#Container Insights/##Monitor CCP.md

symptom: AKS built-in services lose access to MCR proxy after customer adds security appliance (e.g. Zscaler) to production environment; image pulls from MCR fail
rootCause: Security appliances intercept/block traffic to MCR proxy endpoints. The outbound IPs of the security appliance are not whitelisted on the MCR proxy side, causing connection failures for AKS system components that need to pull images.
solution: 1) Ask customer to provide their outbound IP ranges (from the security appliance). 2) Contact AKS PG (Andy Zhang) to whitelist those IP ranges on MCR proxy. Process is simple email request. MCR proxy sits between AKS nodes and MCR, providing cached image access in sovereign clouds.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/#Troubleshooting Guides/How to whitelist IP ranges on MCR proxy.md

symptom: Blobfuse2 static mount fails on AKS with permission errors; pod cannot access Azure Blob storage container via managed identity
rootCause: For blobfuse2 static mount using MSI auth, the AKS kubelet identity (named xxx-agentpool) must have 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role on the storage account. For dynamic provisioning, the same role is required on the resource group of the storage account. Missing RBAC assignment causes mount failures.
solution: 1) Identify kubelet identity: az aks show -> identityProfile.kubeletidentity.clientId. 2) For static mount: assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' on storage account. 3) For dynamic mount: assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' on resource group. 4) StorageClass must specify AzureStorageAuthType: MSI and AzureStorageIdentityClientID with kubelet identity client ID. 5) Create SC -> PV (with volumeHandle, resourceGroup, storageAccount, containerName) -> PVC -> Pod. Ref: github.com/kubernetes-sigs/blob-csi-driver/blob/master/deploy/example/blobfuse-mi/README.md
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/[Blobfuse2]AKS_Static mount Azure Blob storage in.md

symptom: AKS cluster provision fails when VNET uses custom DNS server that does not open UDP port 53
rootCause: Custom DNS server configured on the VNET did not have UDP port 53 open, causing DNS query timeouts during AKS provisioning. AKS node bootstrapping requires DNS resolution to reach Azure endpoints.
solution: Ensure the custom DNS server opens UDP port 53 for DNS queries. Verify DNS resolution works from within the VNET before creating AKS cluster.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2018_12_18 - 2019_1_5.md

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with CNI address range validation error; Kubernetes service address range uses restricted CIDR blocks
rootCause: AKS clusters cannot use 169.254.0.0/16, 172.30.0.0/16, or 172.31.0.0/16 for the Kubernetes service address range. These are reserved ranges.
solution: Use address ranges outside the restricted blocks. Best practice: Node IP from VNET subnet address, Service IP in a different range, Cluster IP (pod CIDR) in another separate range.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2018_12_18 - 2019_1_5.md

symptom: AKS cannot create internal load balancer; AADSTS70002 error Invalid client secret is provided when attempting ILB creation
rootCause: Service Principal credentials used during AKS cluster creation were invalid or expired. The SP is required for AKS to manage Azure resources including load balancers.
solution: Reset the Service Principal credentials: az aks update-credentials or recreate the SP. Verify SP has Contributor role on the AKS resource group.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2018_12_18 - 2019_1_5.md

symptom: AKS creation via CLI stuck in loop when deploying into existing VNET; cluster never finishes provisioning
rootCause: Deploying AKS into an existing VNET via CLI without specifying --network-plugin azure defaults to kubenet, which causes deployment issues when using a pre-existing subnet.
solution: Add --network-plugin azure flag when creating AKS cluster in existing VNET: az aks create --resource-group myRG --name myAKS --network-plugin azure --vnet-subnet-id <subnet-id>
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2018_12_18 - 2019_1_5.md

symptom: Docker daemon stops working on AKS nodes; kubelet logs show Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock; node reports OOM
rootCause: Cluster running outdated Docker version (docker://1.13.1) combined with OOM conditions on the node. Old Docker runtime is less resilient to memory pressure.
solution: Update Docker/Moby to version 3.0.x or later. If node is in failed state, resize the OS disk: az disk update -g $nodeRG -n $OSdiskname -z $newSize. Drain and cordon affected nodes before maintenance.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_1_16 - 2019_1_22.md

symptom: Virtual-node-aci-linux pod deployment stays in Pending state; pod logs show resource not found error referencing ContainerInstance container group API
rootCause: ACI virtual node integration issue where the container group resource is not found in the specified resource group. May be caused by region mismatch, missing ACI provider registration, or virtual node add-on misconfiguration.
solution: Verify ACI provider is registered (az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerInstance). Check virtual node add-on is properly enabled. Ensure ACI region matches AKS region.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_1_16 - 2019_1_22.md

symptom: Cannot change subnet CIDR or IP address range for existing AKS cluster without recreating the cluster
rootCause: Once AKS cluster is created, subnet CIDR and VNET IP configuration cannot be changed directly. This is a platform limitation.
solution: Workaround: 1) Remove/move nodes from the subnet 2) Change the CIDR 3) Go to resources.azure.com, do GET then PUT on the cluster to re-create nodes 4) Re-apply YAML files with updated CIDR if workloads reference original CIDR.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_1_23 - 2019_1_29.md

symptom: DNS resolution in AKS pods fails intermittently; sporadic DNS lookup timeouts inside containers
rootCause: Known Linux (Ubuntu) kernel race condition in conntrack/netfilter causing intermittent DNS query failures. The race condition affects UDP DNS packets traversing iptables NAT rules.
solution: Upgrade to a newer AKS node image with patched kernel. Workaround: use TCP for DNS queries, or configure ndots:1 in pod DNS config to reduce DNS query amplification.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_1_23 - 2019_1_29.md

symptom: AKS cluster stuck in Failed state due to failed VM Extension AksLinuxBilling; provisioning state shows TemplateDeploymentTimedoutError
rootCause: The AksLinuxBilling VM extension on one of the agent nodes failed or timed out during deployment, causing the entire cluster to enter a Failed provisioning state.
solution: After the extension recovers to healthy state, reset the cluster: 1) Go to resources.azure.com 2) Navigate to the AKS cluster resource 3) Perform GET then PUT to reconcile the cluster state.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_1_9 - 2019_1_15.md

symptom: cert-manager helm install fails with no matches for kind Certificate in version certmanager.k8s.io/v1alpha1; ClusterIssuer not found
rootCause: cert-manager Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) were not installed before the Helm chart. New cert-manager releases require CRDs to be applied separately first. Additionally, a ClusterIssuer resource must exist before Certificate resources can reference it.
solution: Install CRDs first: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jetstack/cert-manager/release-0.6/deploy/manifests/00-crds.yaml. Then create ClusterIssuer resource with name letsencrypt-prod before deploying certificates.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_2_13 - 2019_2_19.md

symptom: Kubernetes node stops scheduling new pods but node status shows Ready/Healthy; existing pods run fine but no new pods are placed on the node
rootCause: Node may be cordoned (SchedulingDisabled), have taints preventing scheduling, or have resource pressure (CPU/memory) that prevents new pod admission despite appearing healthy.
solution: Check node status: kubectl describe node <name>. Look for taints, cordoned status, or resource pressure conditions. Uncordon if needed: kubectl uncordon <node>. Remove problematic taints if applicable.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_2_13 - 2019_2_19.md

symptom: Helm commands fail with no available release name found or could not find tiller in RBAC-enabled AKS cluster
rootCause: RBAC-enabled AKS cluster requires a dedicated service account and ClusterRoleBinding for the Tiller service. Without proper RBAC setup, Tiller cannot manage releases.
solution: Create a service account for Tiller with proper RBAC bindings per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/kubernetes-helm#create-a-service-account. Then initialize helm with: helm init --service-account tiller.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##AKS Readiness/Case Review_2019_2_20 - 2019_2_26.md

symptom: Attempting to disable SSH access on AKS cluster fails with error DisableSSHNotSupportedOnVMASAgentPool when the agent pool is VMAS type
rootCause: The --ssh-access disabled feature is only supported on VMSS-based agent pools. VMAS (Virtual Machine Availability Set) agent pools do not support this configuration.
solution: Feature limitation. Only VMSS-based agent pools support disabling SSH. Migrate agent pool to VMSS type if SSH disabling is required. See https://aka.ms/aks/disable-ssh
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_08_21.md

symptom: Node labels added using kubectl label are removed/lost after AKS node image upgrade
rootCause: During node image upgrade, the node is deleted from the API server and reimaged with the same node name. When the node is deleted from API server, its data (including custom labels) in etcd is cleaned. The new node registers without the custom labels.
solution: Use AKS-managed node pool labels (--labels parameter in az aks nodepool add/update) instead of kubectl label. AKS-managed labels persist across node image upgrades. Behavior confirmed since AKS release 2024-04-28.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_08_8.md

symptom: Docker pull fails with unauthorized: authentication required after about 3 hours when authenticated to ACR via az acr login with service principal
rootCause: az acr login writes an access token with 3-hour TTL to ~/.docker/config.json. Docker commands and az CLI commands do not refresh this token automatically. In contrast, docker login with SP credentials directly does not have this expiration issue.
solution: Option 1: Use docker login directly with SP credentials (--username SP_APP_ID --password SP_PASSWD) to avoid token expiration. Option 2: Re-run az acr login periodically before the 3-hour window expires. Option 3: Set up a cron job or CI/CD step to refresh the token.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_10_31.md

symptom: After customer deleted a VMSS node directly at VMSS level, updating apiServerAuthorizedIPRanges triggers an unexpected nodepool scale operation that re-creates the deleted node
rootCause: AKS reconciliation during PutManagedCluster detects node count mismatch between desired state (stored in AKS RP) and actual VMSS count. When any cluster-level config change (like IP range update) triggers reconciliation, AKS scales the nodepool back to desired count.
solution: Do not delete VMSS nodes directly at VMSS level. Use AKS node management (az aks nodepool scale) to change node count. If already in this state, use az aks nodepool scale to set the correct desired count before making other cluster config changes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_12_11.md

symptom: Updating AKS APIServerAuthorizedIPRanges triggers unexpected node pool scaling; cluster reconciles node count and scales up when VMSS nodes were previously manually deleted
rootCause: When a customer manually deletes a VMSS instance, AKS RP still records the original node count. Any subsequent PutManagedCluster operation (e.g., updating authorized IP ranges) triggers a full reconciliation which detects the node count mismatch and initiates scaling to restore the expected count.
solution: 1) Never delete VMSS instances manually; use az aks nodepool scale or az aks nodepool delete-machines (GA). 2) If nodes were already manually deleted, be aware that any cluster update (IP range, tags, etc.) will trigger reconciliation and scaling. 3) To safely reduce node count after manual deletion, scale down via AKS API first before making other changes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_12_11.md

symptom: AKS StatefulSet pod is evicted ~5 minutes after VM shutdown (by design), but Kubernetes endpoint does not update as expected; service still points to terminated pod
rootCause: When a VM is shut down, the node becomes NotReady after node-monitor-grace-period. Pod is evicted after pod-eviction-timeout (~5 min by default). However, endpoint controller update timing may lag behind pod eviction, especially for StatefulSets which have stricter lifecycle guarantees.
solution: Investigate endpoint controller logs for delays. Check if readiness probes are correctly configured. For StatefulSets, consider using headless services and verify pod readiness probe configuration. This behavior needs further investigation with specific cluster logs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_9_19.md

symptom: AKS node upgrade fails or hangs due to nodes that cannot be drained (e.g., PDB prevents eviction); default behavior causes entire upgrade to fail leaving undrained nodes schedulable
rootCause: Default AKS upgrade drain failure behavior is Schedule mode: if a node cannot be drained, the upgrade operation fails and undrained nodes remain schedulable. This can block upgrades when PodDisruptionBudgets or other constraints prevent pod eviction.
solution: Use the new Cordon behavior (preview): configure upgrade to skip undrained nodes by quarantining them with label kubernetes.azure.com/upgrade-status:Quarantined and proceed with upgrading remaining nodes. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-cluster. After upgrade, troubleshoot quarantined nodes separately.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2024_11_14.md

symptom: AKS App Routing addon 创建的 Ingress Controller 的 PDB/ConfigMap 无法修改或删除，修改后几分钟内自动重置为默认值
rootCause: App-routing operator 通过 reconciliation loop 持续管理 PDB 和 ConfigMap 资源。CRD (nginxingresscontrollers.yaml) 未暴露 PDB/ConfigMap 配置项，用户修改会被 operator 覆盖。删除 Ingress Controller 时 PDB/ConfigMap 随之删除
solution: 这是 app-routing addon 的设计限制。1) PDB 策略和 ConfigMap (如 proxy_pass whitelist) 无法通过直接编辑生效；2) 需自定义 PDB/ConfigMap 时，使用非 addon 方式 (helm) 部署 Ingress Controller；3) 自定义 CA 功能尚无 ETA
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2025_3_11.md

symptom: AKS Remediator 报错 AADSTS500011 invalid_resource，节点上 ManagedIdentityCredential 获取 token 失败，错误信息: error getting token from msi-connector: invalid_resource
rootCause: MSI connector 返回 invalid_resource 错误，SPn token 转换失败。通常因 AAD 应用注册的 resource URI 与实际请求的资源不匹配，或 MSI 配置异常
solution: 1) Kusto 诊断: cluster('mcakshuba.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn').database('AKSprod').CPRemediatorLogs | where level=='error' | where msg contains 'AADSTS500011' | parse msg with * 'node: ' node:string ': ManagedIdentityCredential' *; 2) 检查节点 MSI 配置和 AAD 注册; 3) 如需确认受影响节点: distinct node
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2025_4_3.md

symptom: AKS 升级期间 Cluster Autoscaler (CAS) 错误 taint 正常工作节点，导致节点持续被 taint 的死循环，集群可用容量降低
rootCause: 升级期间 AKS 给节点添加 'do not scale down' annotation 防止被 CAS taint。但在 surge node 完全删除前 annotation 被提前移除，CAS 发现当前节点数(3) > mincount(2) 后 taint 了正常节点。此后 taint 不会被自动移除，形成死循环。Pod 仍可调度到 tainted node，但优先级降低
solution: 已知 bug (ICM-588873918)。1) 等待 PG 修复; 2) 临时方案：手动移除节点上的 CAS taint; 3) 注意：tainted node 上的 pod 不会被驱逐，但新 pod 优先调度到其他节点
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY25/2025_4_3.md

symptom: CNI Overlay AKS 集群中 IP 不足时，尝试使用同 VNET 新 subnet 添加 node pool 报错 GetVnetError: Get virtual network failed (resource group: MC_xxx)
rootCause: AKS 在 MC_ 资源组中查找 virtual network 但 CNI Overlay 模式下 VNET 不在 MC_ 资源组中，导致找不到。错误信息中 virtual network name 为空，表明 AKS 无法解析 VNET 引用
solution: 1) 参考文档 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/node-pool-unique-subnet 正确配置; 2) 确保 --vnet-subnet-id 指定完整的 subnet resource ID（包含正确的 subscription/resourceGroup/vnet/subnet 路径）; 3) 确认 AKS SP/MI 对目标 VNET 有 Network Contributor 权限
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY26/2025_12_25.md

symptom: AKS 集群启用 OIDC Issuer 后无法禁用，客户要求 disable OIDC 功能
rootCause: OIDC Issuer 一旦启用即为不可逆操作 (by design)。禁用 OIDC 会破坏依赖它的 Workload Identity、Service Account Token 等功能，影响面广泛。AKS 文档明确标注此限制
solution: 1) 这是 by design，无法 disable; 2) 如客户坚持要禁用，唯一方案是创建新集群; 3) 启用前应评估影响; 4) 文档: https://docs.azure.cn/en-us/aks/use-oidc-issuer#update-an-aks-cluster-with-oidc-issuer
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY26/2025_8_21.md

symptom: 客户 AKS 集群使用已超出 LTS 支持窗口的 Kubernetes 版本（如 1.29），询问是否仍能获得支持
rootCause: AKS LTS 版本有明确的支持时间窗口，超出后不再获得平台级别支持。Free tier 集群不享受 LTS 支持
solution: 1) 与 PG 协商延长特定集群的支持期限; 2) 在 ICM comments 中注明: 'Synced with PG and will extend platform level support for these clusters till {date}'; 3) 邮件中告知客户升级计划和时间表; 4) 期间如遇 support 无法解决的问题仍可开 ICM
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##FY26/2025_8_21.md

symptom: AKS LoadBalancer Service stuck in <pending> state; describe svc shows 401 AADSTS7000222 'client secret keys are expired'; azure-cloud-provider cannot list/sync load balancers
rootCause: AKS cluster's Service Principal client secret expired. All ARM API calls from cloud-provider-azure fail with HTTP 401. LoadBalancer, PublicIP, and other Azure resource reconciliation is blocked.
solution: 1) Reset SP credentials: az ad sp credential reset --id <appId>; 2) Update AKS: az aks update-credentials --resource-group <rg> --name <aks> --reset-service-principal --service-principal-id <appId> --client-secret <newSecret>; 3) Consider migrating to Managed Identity to avoid SP expiry issues.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##MOC Lab/Mock Lab1.md

symptom: AKS pod fails to mount Azure Files (CSI driver) with mount error; dynamic provisioning of Azure File share PV fails; pod stuck in ContainerCreating
rootCause: NSG on the AKS subnet blocks outbound TCP port 445 (SMB) to the storage account. Azure Files requires port 445 for SMB mounting. If NSG rules deny this traffic, the CSI driver cannot mount the file share.
solution: 1) Check NSG rules on AKS node subnet: ensure outbound TCP 445 is allowed to storage account IP or AzureStorage service tag; 2) Verify from AKS node: nc -vz <storage-account>.file.core.chinacloudapi.cn 445; 3) If port 445 is blocked by corporate firewall, consider using Azure Files NFS (port 2049) or Azure Blob CSI driver instead.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##MOC Lab/Mock Lab4.md

symptom: AKS cluster with custom vnet + kubenet network plugin has network connectivity issues; pods cannot reach external services or other pods across nodes
rootCause: When using custom vnet with kubenet, customer did not follow the doc to properly configure routeTable and NSG on the subnet. Kubenet requires a user-defined route table and appropriate NSG rules for pod-to-pod communication across nodes.
solution: 1) Follow official doc to associate route table and NSG with subnet: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/aks/configure-kubenet#associate-network-resources-with-the-node-subnet; 2) Ensure the route table created by AKS is associated to the node subnet; 3) NSG must allow inter-node traffic for pod CIDR range; 4) Consider using Azure CNI if kubenet config is too complex.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY19/20190321.md

symptom: AKS cluster created via Azure Portal in Mooncake shows Standard Load Balancer SKU during configuration, but after cluster creation az aks show reveals the cluster actually uses Basic Load Balancer. Ingress controller with static public IP fails because Basic LB does not support static IP assignment.
rootCause: Product bug in Mooncake AKS portal: the UI incorrectly displays Standard LB as selected, but the backend creates a Basic LB. This was a known platform issue affecting multiple customers.
solution: 1) Verify LB SKU with: az aks show -g <rg> -n <cluster> --query networkProfile.loadBalancerSku. 2) If Basic, recreate cluster explicitly specifying --load-balancer-sku standard in az aks create. 3) This was eventually fixed by PG deployment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200109.md

symptom: AKS cluster deletion fails or hangs; customer cannot delete AKS cluster from portal or CLI.
rootCause: Failure of deleting underlying VM (in MC_ resource group) blocks the cluster deletion pipeline. The cluster remains in a stuck/failed state.
solution: 1) Manually delete the blocking VM in the MC_ resource group first. 2) After VM is deleted, retry cluster deletion. 3) If still fails, contact AKS PG to perform cluster reconcile, then retry delete.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY19/20190516.md

symptom: chmod command does not take effect on Azure File share mounted in AKS pod. Customer tries chmod 777 or other permission changes but file permissions remain unchanged.
rootCause: Work by design. Azure Files (SMB) does not support POSIX file permission changes via chmod. All files inherit the mount-level permissions specified in mountOptions (fileMode/dirMode).
solution: 1) Set file/dir permissions at mount time using mountOptions in PV/StorageClass: fileMode=0777, dirMode=0777. 2) Cannot change permissions after mount. 3) If POSIX permissions needed, consider Azure Files NFS or Azure Disk instead.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20191031.md

symptom: AKS VMSS-based cluster experiences ARM API throttling (HTTP 429 errors) causing scale operations, disk attach/detach, and LoadBalancer service creation to fail or hang.
rootCause: High frequency of ARM API calls from VMSS cluster operations (node scaling, disk operations, LB changes) exceeds ARM throttling limits.
solution: To reduce ARM API requests: 1) Reduce number of nodes and frequency of node scaling operations. 2) Reduce frequency of AzureDisk attach/detach operations. 3) Reduce frequency of LoadBalancer-type service creation/deletion. 4) See https://github.com/Azure/AKS/issues/1187 for details.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20191226.md

symptom: Azure CLI commands for AKS fail in Mooncake with API version errors or unexpected behavior. E.g. Use Azure CLI cannot get the kubeconfig.
rootCause: Azure CLI version is too new/high for the Mooncake API version. Newer CLI versions default to ARM API versions not yet available in Mooncake sovereign cloud.
solution: 1) Downgrade Azure CLI to a version compatible with Mooncake API. 2) Specify --cloud AzureChinaCloud explicitly. 3) Avoid using preview CLI extensions (akspreview) in Mooncake unless confirmed supported.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200206.md

symptom: Terraform AKS deployment fails with error: Addon kubeDashboard is not supported in this cloud environment. Happens when creating AKS cluster in Mooncake using Terraform.
rootCause: The kubeDashboard addon is not available in Mooncake (Azure China). Terraform templates that include kubeDashboard addon configuration will fail.
solution: 1) Remove kubeDashboard addon from Terraform AKS resource definition. 2) Use kubectl proxy or az aks browse for dashboard access instead. 3) kubeDashboard is deprecated in newer Kubernetes versions globally.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200220.md

symptom: HTTP Application Routing addon shows not applicable or cannot be enabled in AKS cluster in Mooncake. Feature appears disabled in portal.
rootCause: HTTP Application Routing is not supported in Mooncake (Azure China). This is a known feature gap between global Azure and Mooncake.
solution: 1) Do not use HTTP Application Routing in Mooncake. 2) Use nginx-ingress controller (helm install stable/nginx-ingress) as alternative. 3) For custom DNS, manually configure DNS records + ingress rules.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20191212.md

symptom: Customer modifies kernel parameters (sysctl) on AKS node VM, but changes do not take effect inside AKS pods. Container cannot use host kernel parameters.
rootCause: Work by design. AKS pods run in isolated namespaces and do not inherit all host kernel parameters. Sysctl settings on the node only apply to host-level processes, not containerized workloads, unless explicitly allowed.
solution: 1) Use Kubernetes allowed-unsafe-sysctls or safe sysctls in pod security context for supported parameters. 2) For AKS, configure allowed sysctls via --custom-node-config at cluster/nodepool creation. 3) Not all kernel parameters can be changed in containers by design.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20191226.md

symptom: kube-svc-redirect pod shows frequent restarts (CrashLoopBackOff) in AKS cluster. Pod restart count keeps increasing.
rootCause: Product issue with kube-svc-redirect component. This was a known product-level bug affecting certain AKS versions.
solution: 1) Check AKS version and upgrade to latest supported version. 2) If issue persists after upgrade, contact AKS PG for investigation. 3) This component handles service traffic redirection; frequent restarts may intermittently affect service routing.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20191212.md

symptom: AKS monitoring in Mooncake is missing features compared to global Azure. InsightsMetrics table queries fail or return no data. Azure Monitor Containers tab not visible. Alert rules on AKS node metrics cannot be created.
rootCause: Mooncake Azure Monitor lacks Azure Resource Graph (ARG) backend support, which is required for the Containers tab. InsightsMetrics table is a preview feature not available in Mooncake. Feature gap between global and sovereign cloud.
solution: 1) Use ContainerLog and Perf tables in Log Analytics for monitoring instead of InsightsMetrics. 2) For alerting, use log-based alerts on KubePodInventory/KubeNodeInventory tables. 3) Check Mooncake feature availability docs for latest updates.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY19/20190530.md

symptom: Docker image pull fails with image not found when using Mooncake mirror dockerhub.azk8s.cn. For example, pulling dockerhub.azk8s.cn/nginx:1.15.5 fails.
rootCause: Mooncake container mirror (dockerhub.azk8s.cn) requires explicit library/ prefix for official Docker Hub images. Unlike Docker Hub which implicitly adds library/ for official images, the mirror does not.
solution: Use full path with library/ prefix: dockerhub.azk8s.cn/library/nginx:1.15.5 instead of dockerhub.azk8s.cn/nginx:1.15.5. For non-official images, use the full namespace as-is.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY19/20190516.md

symptom: Open Service Broker for Azure (OSBA) helm install succeeds but broker fails to provision Azure resources in Mooncake. Services created via OSBA return errors or target wrong environment.
rootCause: OSBA defaults to global Azure environment. Without specifying azure.environment=AzureChinaCloud, the broker attempts to use global Azure endpoints which are not accessible from Mooncake.
solution: Add --set azure.environment=AzureChinaCloud to the helm install command for OSBA in Mooncake.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY19/20190516.md

symptom: After deleting Log Analytics workspace, AKS cluster scaling operations fail. Cluster enters error state and cannot add/remove nodes.
rootCause: AKS cluster has a dependency on the associated Log Analytics workspace for the monitoring addon. Deleting the workspace while AKS monitoring addon is still enabled breaks the ARM template deployment for cluster operations.
solution: 1) Disable monitoring addon before deleting workspace: az aks disable-addons -a monitoring -g <rg> -n <cluster>. 2) If already deleted, recreate the workspace with same name in same resource group, or disable the monitoring addon to unblock cluster operations. 3) Then re-enable monitoring with a new workspace.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20191226.md

symptom: AKS managed cluster control plane becomes deallocated after customer stops/deallocates all agent nodes for more than 30 days. Cluster operations fail; kubectl commands cannot reach API server.
rootCause: By design, if all AKS agent nodes are deallocated for 30+ consecutive days, the managed control plane is automatically deallocated to save resources. This is documented in AKS support policies.
solution: Validate deallocation: use Get Managed Cluster Geneva Action with UnVersioned API version, check if deallocationStatus field exists. Reallocate: perform any PUT operation on the cluster (e.g., add nodes/scale up). Prevention: ensure at least one agent node is running. TSG: CloudNativeCompute wiki/38920/TSG-Managed-Cluster-Deallocation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200611.md

symptom: CoreDNS in AKS Mooncake cluster is upgraded by platform without prior customer notification, causing DNS resolution failures in production workloads.
rootCause: AKS platform performed CoreDNS component upgrade. CXP confirmed notification was not sent to Mooncake customers. ICM 179932700 filed. After incident, Service Health was updated to include AKS notifications.
solution: Post-incident: Service Health now includes AKS. Mitigation: monitor CoreDNS pod restarts (kubectl get pods -n kube-system -w). For CSS: if customer reports sudden DNS failures after platform maintenance, check CoreDNS pod versions and recent restart times.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200514.md

symptom: Azure CLI version incompatibility in Mooncake for AKS management. Az CLI 2.2.0 has functional issues; Az CLI 2.3.0 does not support Mooncake. Az CLI >= 2.3.1 required for system/user nodepool management.
rootCause: Az CLI releases have Mooncake compatibility gaps. Certain API versions or features require specific CLI versions that may not be available or fully functional in Mooncake at the same time as global Azure.
solution: For system/user nodepool management: use Az CLI >= 2.3.1. If aks-preview extension is installed, ensure it is updated to latest: az extension update --name aks-preview. Check CLI version compatibility: az version. Report Mooncake-specific CLI issues to GitHub.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200430.md

symptom: AKS node shows NotReady status. Node cannot communicate with API server; error indicates API server domain name resolution failure.
rootCause: AKS agent node fails to resolve API server FQDN. Root cause varies: custom DNS misconfiguration, DNS forwarding chain broken, VNet DNS settings pointing to unreachable server, or transient Azure DNS issue.
solution: SSH/exec into node and test DNS: nslookup <api-server-fqdn>. Check VNet DNS settings ensure custom DNS can resolve AKS API server FQDN. Verify DNS forwarding to 168.63.129.16. Check /etc/resolv.conf. If persistent, cordon/drain and recreate the node.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200402.md

symptom: Terraform unable to create NSG for AKS subnet. Long-aged case pending on network PG across multiple PG sync meetings (March-July 2020).
rootCause: Product issue / by-design limitation. AKS manages its own NSG on the node subnet; creating a separate NSG via Terraform conflicts with AKS-managed NSG.
solution: Do not create custom NSG directly on AKS node subnet if using AKS-managed networking. Use AKS network policy (Calico/Azure) for pod-level traffic control. For custom NSG, apply rules on AKS-managed NSG in MC_ resource group. Escalate to networking PG if needed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200319.md

symptom: AKS system nodepool constraints: cannot delete last system nodepool, system pods not exclusively running on system nodepool, Basic LB with multiple nodepools not supported.
rootCause: By design: AKS requires at least one system nodepool with min 1 node. User nodepools can scale to 0. System pods like CoreDNS may be scheduled on any nodepool. Basic LB does not support multiple nodepools.
solution: System nodepool must have >= 1 node; use Az CLI >= 2.3.1. User pool can scale to 0 via CLI. To isolate system pods, use node taints/tolerations (CriticalAddonsOnly). For multiple nodepools, use Standard LB. az aks nodepool update --mode System.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200611.md

symptom: AKS pod CPU rapidly spikes causing pod eviction (Evicted status). Multiple pods evicted simultaneously on same node.
rootCause: Pod resource limits not properly configured. When pod CPU usage spikes beyond node capacity, kubelet evicts pods to protect node stability. Standard Kubernetes eviction behavior triggered by node resource pressure.
solution: Set proper resource requests and limits in pod spec. Check node resource pressure: kubectl describe node. Review eviction events: kubectl get events --field-selector reason=Evicted. Consider PodDisruptionBudget. Scale out nodepool if persistent.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200514.md

symptom: metrics-server pod in AKS cluster continuously crashes (CrashLoopBackOff). Kubernetes dashboard returns HTTP 500 errors when accessing metrics.
rootCause: Product issue with metrics-server component. metrics-server pod unable to collect node/pod metrics, possibly due to network policy blocking, API server connectivity, or component version bug.
solution: Check metrics-server logs: kubectl logs -n kube-system -l k8s-app=metrics-server. Verify API server aggregation: kubectl get apiservice v1beta1.metrics.k8s.io. Check network policy not blocking port 10250. Restart: kubectl rollout restart deployment metrics-server -n kube-system. Engage AKS PG if product issue.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY20/20200528.md

symptom: AKS does not support kubelet configuration such as Hard/Soft Eviction Thresholds
rootCause: AKS managed service does not expose kubelet configuration parameters by default (as of 2020)
solution: Develop a DaemonSet to apply custom kubelet config on each node. Later AKS versions support --kubelet-config flag
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200806.md

symptom: Container images from us.gcr.io not accessible in Azure China (Mooncake); AKS deployments fail
rootCause: Google Container Registry domains blocked or unreliable from China network
solution: Use Azure China proxy usgcr.azk8s.cn. Replace us.gcr.io/repo/image:tag with usgcr.azk8s.cn/repo/image:tag
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200806.md

symptom: AKS agent node auto-repair (Remediator) causes Docker service to go down on agent node
rootCause: Remediator on old underlay cluster version has incorrect auto-repair behavior
solution: After underlay cluster migration, Remediator behaves correctly. Ensure cluster is on updated underlay
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200820.md

symptom: Cannot modify CoreDNS forward plugin policy for root zone in AKS; need to change from random to sequential
rootCause: AKS does not allow direct modification of the built-in CoreDNS Corefile
solution: Configure a custom CoreDNS ConfigMap to override or extend DNS behavior. Do not modify built-in corefile directly
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200820.md

symptom: LoadBalancer Service takes hours for external IP assignment; Service remains in Pending state
rootCause: Known upstream Kubernetes issue with LoadBalancer IP allocation mechanism
solution: Upgrade to Kubernetes 1.18.2+ which includes upstream fix (PR #88699)
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200820.md

symptom: kubectl fails with Unable to connect to the server: net/http: TLS handshake timeout
rootCause: Network connectivity issue between client and AKS API server
solution: Check network connectivity, DNS resolution, firewall/NSG rules, API server authorized IP ranges
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200917.md

symptom: AKS version upgrade blocked by PodDisruptionBudget (PDB) settings; hangs during node drain
rootCause: PDB with minAvailable too high or maxUnavailable=0 prevents node drain during upgrade
solution: Adjust PDB: set maxUnavailable >= 1 or lower minAvailable. Or temporarily delete PDB during upgrade
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200917.md

symptom: AKS nodepool shows user mode in portal but is actually system mode; persists after upgrade from 1.15.x
rootCause: Bug in AKS 1.15.x where nodepool mode metadata was not correctly set; persists after upgrade
solution: File ICM for PG fix. Verify via label kubernetes.azure.com/mode: system. New clusters on 1.16+ not affected
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20200917.md

symptom: After AKS underlay cluster migration, DNS records keep resetting to original gateway IP
rootCause: Legacy external-dns deployment was not scaled down during migration
solution: Scale down or delete legacy external-dns deployment after migration
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20201015.md

symptom: After AKS underlay migration, API server IP did not change; cluster behavior inconsistent
rootCause: Cluster service principal expired before migration, preventing IP update
solution: Ensure SP is valid before maintenance/migration. Renew via az aks update-credentials if expired
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20201015.md

symptom: Azure NPM (Network Policy Manager) pods crash with OOM in AKS when network policy is enabled
rootCause: NPM pods consume excessive memory, exceeding default limits and getting OOM-killed
solution: Workaround: disable network policy. Check with PG for updated NPM version with memory optimization
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20201015.md

symptom: AKS pods experience intermittent 5-second DNS resolution delays
rootCause: Race condition in Linux kernel with concurrent UDP DNS queries on conntrack entries
solution: Update Azure CNI. Options: single-request-reopen in resolv.conf, node-local DNS cache, or upgrade kernel. TSG: CloudNativeCompute wiki/101057
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20201210.md

symptom: AKS control plane resource limits unknown; API server perf issues under heavy load
rootCause: With Uptime SLA, master nodes get fixed CPU/Mem with 12GB memory limit for control plane pods
solution: Enable AKS Uptime SLA for fixed control plane resources (12GB mem). Without SLA, allocation is best-effort
sourceRef: POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20201126.md

symptom: AKS cluster scale-up fails with error 'The deployment size has exceeded internal system limitations' during node pool scale out (East Money customer, Mooncake)
rootCause: Internal VMSS deployment size limit hit during scale-up operation; large cluster or subscription-level resource constraints cause ARM deployment to exceed system limits
solution: Contact AKS PG oncall directly for workaround (PG may adjust internal limit or provide alternative scaling approach). ICM reference: 230124446
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20210304.md

symptom: After upgrading AKS to Kubernetes 1.19.7, Azure Files volume mount fails; pod searches for storage account secret in 'default' namespace instead of its own namespace
rootCause: Bug in Kubernetes 1.19.7 broke Azure Files CSI driver namespace lookup logic - secret namespace resolution defaults to 'default' instead of pod namespace (GitHub Azure/AKS#2108)
solution: Upgrade to a patched AKS 1.19.x version; or manually create the Azure Files storage secret in the pod namespace as workaround. Track fix via GitHub Azure/AKS#2108
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20210401.md

symptom: AKS VMSS and VM instances show different image versions (e.g. VMSS model sku 2021-q1 vs VM instance sku 2020-05); orchestrator version tag missing on VMSS triggers unexpected node image update
rootCause: When orchestrator version tag does not exist or is nil on older VMSS, AKS reconciler detects model mismatch and attempts to update VMSS model, triggering unplanned node image updates
solution: Monitor VMSS model vs instance image versions; for older clusters without tags, reconciliation may trigger updates during upgrade or node operations. Newer AKS versions properly set orchestrator version tags
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/20210318.md

symptom: Updating AKS cluster Service Principal credentials triggers a reboot of all worker nodes in the cluster, causing service disruption
rootCause: SP credential update writes new credentials to /etc/kubernetes/azure.json on each node via VMSS model update, which triggers node reimaging and reboot of all instances
solution: 1) Plan SP credential rotation during maintenance window. 2) Migrate from SP to Managed Identity (MSI) - GA in Mooncake with CLI >= 2.24. MSI eliminates SP rotation entirely. 3) For graceful SP update without full reboot, see aks-onenote-150
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/202104029.md

symptom: Azure Portal cannot enable Container Insights (Monitoring addon) for AKS cluster in Mooncake; Portal call uses outdated API version 2019-11-01 which does not support the addon
rootCause: Mooncake Portal had not updated AKS resource provider API version; Portal uses api-version=2019-11-01 while CLI uses 2021-03-01. The older API version does not expose monitoring addon configuration
solution: Use Azure CLI to enable Container Insights: 'az aks enable-addons -a monitoring -n <cluster> -g <rg>'. CLI uses newer API versions that support the addon. Portal fix requires Mooncake Portal team to update AKS blade API version
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/2021_06_09.md

symptom: AKS cluster scale-up or node pool addition fails due to CustomScriptExtension (CSE) timeout; new nodes fail to join cluster and remain in NotReady state
rootCause: CSE execution on new VMSS instances exceeds timeout during node bootstrapping, typically caused by network connectivity issues (DNS resolution, package download from MCR/apt repos) or slow provisioning in Mooncake
solution: 1) Check CSE logs on failed node via SSH or serial console. 2) AKS remediator may auto-heal by reimaging the node (CSE -> node not ready -> remediator). 3) If auto-heal fails, manually delete stuck nodes and retry scale-up. 4) Verify DNS and outbound connectivity from VMSS subnet
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/2021_07_15.md

symptom: After manually resizing AKS VMSS to a different VM SKU size, the AKS resource provider configuration still shows old VM size; cluster state drift between VMSS and AKS RP
rootCause: AKS RP tracks VM SKU in its internal state database; manual VMSS resize via Azure Portal or ARM API bypasses AKS RP and causes state drift between the actual VMSS and AKS control plane records
solution: Do NOT manually resize AKS VMSS. Use AKS-supported method: 1) Create new node pool with desired VM SKU. 2) Cordon/drain old nodes. 3) Delete old node pool. TSG available for team reference
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/2021_08_12.md

symptom: AKS image pull fails with timeout or connection refused when cluster uses custom UDR for outbound traffic; MCR endpoint blocks requests from unknown source IPs
rootCause: Customer uses custom UDR routing outbound traffic through NVA/firewall with different egress IP. MCR endpoint blocks requests from unregistered/unknown source IPs not in its allow-list
solution: 1) Whitelist the NVA/firewall egress IP with MCR team (file ICM). 2) Ensure firewall rules allow traffic to mcr.azure.cn (Mooncake). 3) Verify UDR does not block MCR endpoints. See also aks-onenote-102 for MCR FQDN changes
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/2021_06_09.md

symptom: Need to access AKS node host filesystem for troubleshooting but SSH session lands inside container environment
rootCause: AKS node SSH connects to a privileged container, not directly to the host OS. Without chroot, commands run against container filesystem.
solution: After SSH into AKS node (per docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/aks/ssh), run 'chroot /host' to access the actual node filesystem and run host-level diagnostics.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/2021_08_26.md

symptom: AKS cluster created after API version 2020-11-01 unexpectedly uses ephemeral OS disk, causing data loss on node reimaging
rootCause: AKS clusters created with apiVersion >= 2020-11-01 have ephemeral OS disk enabled by default. Ephemeral OS disks do not persist data across node reimages.
solution: Explicitly set osDiskType to Managed if persistent OS disk is required. For new clusters verify osDiskType setting before creation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##Regular Sync up with PG/###FY21/2021_08_26.md

symptom: AKS API server OOMKilled on large cluster (900 nodes) causing control plane unavailability
rootCause: Rapid cluster scale-up (1 to 900 nodes in 10 minutes) combined with pod crashloops generates excessive API server load leading to OOM. Without uptime-SLA API server has limited resources.
solution: 1) Enable uptime-SLA for production clusters with high node count. 2) Scale nodes gradually. 3) Fix pod crashloops to reduce API server pressure. 4) Engage AKS PG via IcM for persistent OOM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##global AKS PG sync/2021-03-18 Morning.md

symptom: Cannot move AKS VNet or route table to a different resource group after cluster creation
rootCause: AKS cloud provider config (cloudconfig) is hard-coded in RP at cluster creation time referencing specific resource group paths. Moving VNet route table or related resources breaks the reference.
solution: Do not move AKS-associated networking resources (VNet, route table, NSG) to other resource groups after cluster creation. Plan resource group layout before creating the cluster.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/##global AKS PG sync/2021-03-18 Morning.md

symptom: AKS private cluster start/stop (preview) leaves private endpoint in disconnected state, cluster cannot reconnect
